description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crab, featuring a broad, slightly domed carapace with a reddish-brown coloration. The crab has two prominent, asymmetrical claws (chelae) at the front, each with a pointed tip and visible articulation. Its body is flanked by four pairs of segmented walking legs on each side, each leg tapering to a fine point and slightly curved, giving the crab a lifelike, dynamic posture. The surface of the carapace is subtly textured, with faint ridges and indentations, and the legs and claws show jointed segments typical of crustaceans. The underside and finer details are not visible, but the overall form suggests a hard exoskeleton typical of marine crabs. No antennae or eyes are distinctly visible in these views, focusing attention on the robust limbs and shell.
description: A reddish-brown crab with a broad, textured shell, two large claws, and eight segmented walking legs, displaying a lifelike, articulated posture.
description_view_0: The crab is seen from a low, frontal angle, highlighting both claws and the full spread of its legs.
description_view_1: A side-rear view emphasizes the curvature and segmentation of the legs, with one claw more prominently visible.
description_view_2: A direct side view shows the symmetrical arrangement of the legs and the flatness of the carapace.
description_view_3: A rear-side view reveals the articulation of the legs and the back of the carapace, with both claws visible but less prominent.
